food_subgroup: Rice-based mixed dishes with meat and broth

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Tom Yum Beef Rice. FOODON_00002809 (beef dish) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (rice-based dish) used as closest parent; no specific code exists for Tom Yum Beef Rice in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept identified for this specific Thai beef rice dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a bowl of steamed jasmine rice topped with beef slices, brisket, and tendon in spicy tom yum broth.

nutrient_source.title: Beef and rice mixed dish — composite estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: Beef, brisket, flat half, separable lean and fat, cooked, braised (NDB 13349); Rice, white, long-grain, cooked (NDB 20444); and Tom Yum soup broth component estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for Thai spicy soup.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FAO INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Tom Yum Beef Rice as a composite dish. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for cooked beef brisket, cooked white rice, and a tom yum broth component, weighted by approximate proportions in a 450 g serving (rice ~200 g, beef components ~150 g, broth ~100 g). Sodium elevated to reflect fish sauce and broth contribution. Values represent a reasonable central estimate; actual restaurant preparation will vary.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice (cooked, jasmine) GI ~68; composite dish GL calculated from carbohydrate content per serving.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tom Yum Beef Rice as a composite dish. GI of 68 is assigned based on the dominant carbohydrate source being cooked jasmine white rice, for which GI ~68 is reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). The beef and broth components contribute negligible glycemic carbohydrate. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (68 × 37.1) / 100 ≈ 25.2, where available carbohydrate per 450 g serving = 16.5 × 4.5 × 0.5 (rice fraction) ≈ 37.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce, broth, and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to an estimated sodium content of approximately 420 mg per 100 g (approximately 1,890 mg per standard 450 g serving), which represents a substantial proportion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; requesting reduced fish sauce and broth volume is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index driven by jasmine white rice as the primary carbohydrate source
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that this dish has a medium GI of approximately 68 and a glycemic load of approximately 25 per serving, primarily from jasmine white rice; portion control is recommended, and substituting brown rice or reducing the rice portion can help moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from beef brisket and tendon, combined with high sodium from broth
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish in moderation due to its saturated fat content from beef brisket and tendon (approximately 1.8 g per 100 g) and its high sodium load from the tom yum broth and fish sauce, both of which are risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes; leaner beef cuts and reduced-sodium preparation are preferable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tom Yum Beef Rice is a Thai comfort dish consisting of steamed jasmine white rice served with a generous portion of tender beef — typically including sliced beef, slow-braised brisket, and gelatinous beef tendon — immersed in or accompanied by a spicy, aromatic tom yum broth. The broth is characteristically sour, spicy, and fragrant, built from lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, fresh chilli, fish sauce, and lime juice, with mushrooms often added for umami depth. The dish is popular in Thai restaurants across Singapore, where it is served as a hearty one-bowl meal. Nutritionally, it provides a moderate energy density of approximately 142 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein from the beef components (approximately 9.2 g per 100 g) and a moderate carbohydrate load from the rice (approximately 16.5 g per 100 g). The dish is high in sodium due to the fish sauce and broth base, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is approximately 68 (medium), driven by the jasmine rice component, and the glycemic load per standard serving is approximately 25, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The beef tendon component contributes collagen and connective tissue protein. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans and contains fish allergens from fish sauce. It is typically dairy-free.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Tom Yum Beef Rice as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for cooked beef brisket, cooked white rice, and a tom yum broth component, weighted by approximate proportions. GI is estimated from the jasmine white rice component using the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite dish in the component sources used (USDA SR Legacy). Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources for this dish. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy beef brisket data and are approximate. The food_name has been normalised from the menu-style name 'T7 Tom Yum Beef Deluxe Rice' to the canonical English name 'Tom Yum Beef Rice'; the original menu name is preserved in aliases. Halal status is marked false as it cannot be confirmed without restaurant certification; the dish may be halal-certified at specific outlets. Sodium estimate of 420 mg per 100 g reflects a typical restaurant preparation with fish sauce and broth; home or reduced-sodium preparations will differ. GL per serving calculated as (68 × 37.1) / 100 ≈ 25.2, where available carbohydrate per 450 g serving is estimated at approximately 37.1 g from the rice fraction of the dish.
